name: Pakistan Personal Data Protection Act
summary: Pakistan's Personal Data Protection Act establishes the country's first comprehensive data privacy framework. It regulates the processing of personal data by public and private entities, introduces consent requirements, creates the National Commission for Personal Data Protection, and sets rules for cross-border data transfers.
keyRequirements: Lawful basis for processing including consent, contractual necessity, and legitimate interest
Data subject rights including access, rectification, erasure, and data portability
Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing activities
Cross-border data transfer restrictions with government-approved adequacy list
National Commission for Personal Data Protection as supervisory authority
Data breach notification to the Commission and affected individuals
penalties: Fines up to PKR 25 million for data controllers and processors. Additional penalties for repeat violations. The National Commission may issue compliance orders, conduct investigations, and impose administrative sanctions.
